title: Burn Injury - Partial Thickness Burns
chief_complaint: 30-year-old male with hot water scald burns to anterior chest and right arm. Severe pain. Burns are red, blistered, and wet. No respiratory distress.

physical_exam.skin: Partial thickness burns to anterior chest (9%) and right arm (9%) = 18% TBSA. Blistered, moist, red, painful. No circumferential burns. No facial burns.

correct_diagnosis: Superficial Partial Thickness Burns (18% TBSA) - Scald
key_learning_points: Parkland formula: 4mL x kg x %TBSA (half in first 8h)
Burn center referral: >10% TBSA partial thickness

patient_responses.burn: I spilled a pot of boiling water on myself

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! 18% TBSA partial thickness burns.
feedback.incorrect_message: These are partial thickness burns. Calculate TBSA and start fluid resuscitation.
feedback.attending_note: 18% TBSA burns: Parkland formula for fluid resuscitation. Silver sulfadiazine dressings. Analgesia. Tetanus prophylaxis. Burn center referral. Monitor for compartment syndrome if circumferential. Good healing expected in 2-3 weeks.
